subject: Gaming
title: Guilds, Clans, and Belonging
description: Learn Guilds, Clans, and Belonging in one minute.

hook.text: Gaming guilds function like real social groups. They have leaders, rules, drama, and deep loyalty.

buildup.text: Maslow's hierarchy places belonging as a fundamental need. Guilds provide identity and community.

discovery.text: Research shows guild members log in more consistently—not for the game, but for their friends.

twist.text: Guild breakups feel like real breakups. Players grieve the loss of their community.

climax.text: For many players, the guild is the game. The social structure matters more than the mechanics.

punchline.text: They log in for friends, not quests. The guild is the real game.

quiz.question: Why do guild members log in more consistently?
options: For social connections with friends, not just the game itself
